title: Odes Of Anacreon - Ode LXXVIII.
content:
В В В В When Cupid sees how thickly now,
В В В В The snows of Time fall o'er my brow,
В В В В Upon his wing of golden light.
В В В В He passes with an eaglet's flight,
В В В В And flitting onward seems to say,
В В В В "Fare thee well, thou'st had thy day!"

В В В В Cupid, whose lamp has lent the ray,
В В В В That lights our life's meandering way,
В В В В That God, within this bosom stealing,
В В В В Hath wakened a strange, mingled feeling.
В В В В Which pleases, though so sadly teasing,
В В В В And teases, though so sweetly pleasing!

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *

В В В В Let me resign this wretched breath
В В В В В В В В Since now remains to me
В В В В No other balm than kindly death,
В В В В В В В В To soothe my misery!

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *

В В В В I know thou lovest a brimming measure,
В В В В В В В В And art a kindly, cordial host;
В В В В But let me fill and drink at pleasure--
В В В В В В В В Thus I enjoy the goblet most.

В В В В I fear that love disturbs my rest,
В В В В В В В В Yet feel not love's impassioned care;
В В В В I think there's madness in my breast
В В В В В В В В Yet cannot find that madness there!

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *

В В В В From dread Leucadia's frowning steep,
В В В В I'll plunge into the whitening deep:
В В В В And there lie cold, to death resigned,
В В В В Since Love intoxicates my mind!

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *

В В В В Mix me, child, a cup divine,
В В В В Crystal water, ruby wine;
В В В В Weave the frontlet, richly flushing
В В В В O'er my wintry temples blushing.
В В В В Mix the brimmer--Love and I
В В В В Shall no more the contest try.
В В В В Here--upon this holy bowl,
В В В В I surrender all my soul!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Moore, titled "Odes Of Anacreon - Ode LXXVIII.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
